reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully captures "A/B is abelian". `[CommGroup A]` matches "abelian group A", and `B : Subgroup A` matches "B is a subgroup of A". The conclusion `∀ a b : A ⧸ B, a*b = b*a` asserts exactly the commutativity of the group operation on the quotient, which is what "A/B is abelian" means. In Mathlib, the quotient `A ⧸ B` automatically has a group structure when `A` is a `CommGroup` (because `Subgroup.normal_of_comm` at `Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Subgroup/Defs.lean:643` makes every subgroup of a commutative group normal), so the multiplication `*` on `A ⧸ B` is well-defined without any additional hypothesis. Thus, asserting `a*b = b*a` for all elements of the quotient is logically equivalent to the informal claim that A/B is abelian.
